brief_document.title: Define durability foundation: error types, configuration, and store trait
brief_document.purpose: Establishes the foundational types that every other durability brief builds on: the error taxonomy for haematite-backed persistence, the per-channel durability configuration (mode selection, partition count, dedup TTL, checkpoint policy), the DurableStore trait that maps directly to haematite's append/read/cas/scan API, and the concrete HaematiteStore implementation. Without these, no durable channel, cursor, dedup, or conversation brief can proceed.
brief_document.task: Create the durability module skeleton and its four foundational files. The error type must cover every failure mode across the durability cluster (haematite errors, sequence conflicts, cursor regression, dedup collisions, configuration errors). The config type must model the four durability modes (ephemeral, durable, durable-dedup, durable-conversation) and their per-channel parameters. The store trait must map 1:1 to haematite's operations — no query builder, no ORM, no abstraction that hides what haematite does (P2). The HaematiteStore implementation delegates directly to haematite's EventStore. Scope boundary: this brief defines types and traits only. It does not implement channel storage, cursor logic, dedup caching, or conversation state — those are DUR-002 through DUR-006.

requirements.title: Define DurabilityError enum with From conversions
requirements.spec: DurabilityError enum SHALL include variants for haematite store errors, sequence conflicts, cursor regression, dedup key collisions, configuration validation errors, and envelope serialization errors. Each variant SHALL carry the context needed for diagnosis (e.g. expected vs actual sequence number for SequenceConflict). The enum SHALL implement std::error::Error via thiserror and SHALL provide From conversions for all haematite error types. DurabilityError SHALL NOT include variants for errors that belong to other clusters (protocol, routing).
acceptance: DurabilityError has at least 6 variants: StoreError, SequenceConflict, CursorRegression, DedupCollision, ConfigError, EnvelopeError.
DurabilityError implements std::error::Error (compiles with `fn foo() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { Err(DurabilityError::ConfigError("test".into()))? }`).
From<haematite_store_error_type> for DurabilityError compiles and maps to StoreError variant.
SequenceConflict variant carries `expected: u64` and `actual: u64` fields.
CursorRegression variant carries `stored: u64` and `attempted: u64` fields.

requirements.title: Define DurabilityConfig with mode, partition, and policy fields
requirements.spec: DurabilityConfig struct SHALL include a DurabilityMode enum (Ephemeral, Durable, DurableDedup, DurableConversation), partition count, dedup TTL (as Duration), and checkpoint policy (PerMessage, PerBatch(usize), ExplicitFlush). All fields SHALL be validated at construction: partition count must be >= 1, dedup TTL must be > Duration::ZERO for DurableDedup mode. DurabilityConfig SHALL NOT include default values for configurable fields — the caller provides all values.
acceptance: DurabilityMode enum has exactly four variants: Ephemeral, Durable, DurableDedup, DurableConversation.
CheckpointPolicy enum has exactly three variants: PerMessage, PerBatch(usize), ExplicitFlush.
DurabilityConfig::new() with partition_count=0 returns Err(DurabilityError::ConfigError).
DurabilityConfig::new() with mode=DurableDedup and dedup_ttl=Duration::ZERO returns Err(DurabilityError::ConfigError).
DurabilityConfig::new() with valid params returns Ok(config) with all fields accessible.

requirements.title: Define DurableStore trait mapping to haematite's API
requirements.spec: DurableStore trait SHALL define four async methods mapping directly to haematite's operations: append(stream_key, payload, expected_seq) -> Result<u64, DurabilityError>, read_from(stream_key, offset, limit) -> Result<Vec<StoredEntry>, DurabilityError>, cas(key, old_value, new_value) -> Result<(), DurabilityError>, and scan(prefix) -> Result<Vec<StoredEntry>, DurabilityError>. The trait SHALL NOT add convenience methods, query builders, or any abstraction beyond what haematite provides. StoredEntry SHALL carry the stored payload bytes, sequence number, and timestamp.
acceptance: DurableStore trait has exactly four methods: append, read_from, cas, scan.
append returns the assigned sequence number on success as Result<u64, DurabilityError>.
read_from takes stream_key, offset (u64), and limit (usize) parameters.
cas takes key, old_value (u64), and new_value (u64) parameters.
StoredEntry struct has fields: payload (Vec<u8>), sequence (u64), timestamp (u64).
DurableStore is object-safe (compiles as `dyn DurableStore`).

requirements.title: Implement HaematiteStore bridging to haematite's EventStore
requirements.spec: HaematiteStore struct SHALL hold a reference to haematite's EventStore and SHALL implement DurableStore by delegating each method directly to the corresponding haematite operation. WHEN append is called with an expected_seq that does not match the stream's current sequence, THE SYSTEM SHALL return DurabilityError::SequenceConflict with the expected and actual values. WHEN cas is called with an old_value that does not match the stored value, THE SYSTEM SHALL return DurabilityError::CursorRegression. HaematiteStore SHALL NOT retry, buffer, or transform haematite responses beyond error mapping.
acceptance: HaematiteStore::new() takes an Arc<haematite::EventStore> (or equivalent handle type) and returns HaematiteStore.
HaematiteStore implements DurableStore.
append delegates to haematite's append and maps SequenceConflict errors.
cas delegates to haematite's cas and maps comparison failures to CursorRegression.
read_from delegates to haematite's read and maps I/O errors to StoreError.
No retry logic, no buffering, no response transformation beyond error mapping.

requirements.title: Wire durability module with pub mod declarations only
requirements.spec: durability/mod.rs SHALL contain only pub mod declarations for error, config, and store submodules, plus pub use re-exports for DurabilityError, DurabilityConfig, DurabilityMode, CheckpointPolicy, DurableStore, HaematiteStore, and StoredEntry. mod.rs SHALL NOT contain any logic, type definitions, trait definitions, or function bodies.
acceptance: mod.rs contains `pub mod error;`, `pub mod config;`, `pub mod store;`.
mod.rs contains pub use re-exports for all public types from submodules.
mod.rs contains zero function bodies, zero struct/enum definitions, zero trait definitions.
The module compiles with `use crate::durability::{DurabilityError, DurabilityConfig, DurableStore, HaematiteStore};`.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement durable channel storage logic — that is DUR-002.
SHALL NOT implement consumer cursor or replay logic — that is DUR-003.
SHALL NOT implement dedup caching or receipt handling — that is DUR-004.
SHALL NOT implement conversation event sourcing — that is DUR-005.
SHALL NOT implement crash recovery orchestration — that is DUR-006.
SHALL NOT add convenience methods or query builders to DurableStore beyond haematite's four operations.
SHALL NOT provide Default implementations for DurabilityConfig — all values are caller-supplied.
verification: cargo check compiles the durability module with no errors.
grep -r 'fn ' crates/liminal/src/durability/mod.rs returns no function definitions.
All public types implement Debug (grep for #[derive(Debug)] or manual Debug impl).
DurabilityError variants cover all error conditions referenced in C1.

adrs.title: Two exactly-once strategies: lightweight dedup contract and heavyweight durable conversations
adrs.decision: Two complementary strategies available per-channel. Lightweight: three-party dedup contract (producer idempotency key with TTL, bus deduplicates, consumer provides processing receipt). Heavyweight: durable conversations (consumer processing state is event-sourced, re-delivery triggers resume not re-execution — idempotency is emergent from resumable processing). The rejected alternative is a single exactly-once strategy (either too heavy for simple consumers or insufficient for complex ones).

adrs.title: Haematite (prolly tree) as the storage backend, not a general-purpose database
adrs.decision: Haematite is the sole storage backend. Content-addressed prolly tree with WAL buffer gives us branchable state, structural merge, hash-based sync, and actor-per-shard write parallelism — all properties that directly benefit the bus (conversation state branching, multi-node sync, zero-contention writes). The rejected alternative is pluggable backends (splits testing surface, dilutes the branchable-state differentiator, and adds abstraction overhead for a benefit that doesn't exist yet).
adrs.decided_by: Bono + Frodo + Sir Patrick Stewart convergence

checklist.text: DurabilityError enum defined in crates/liminal/src/durability/error.rs with variants for haematite errors, sequence conflicts, cursor regression, dedup collisions, and configuration errors.
DurabilityError implements std::error::Error via thiserror with From conversions for all haematite error types.

DurabilityConfig struct defined in crates/liminal/src/durability/config.rs with fields for durability mode (ephemeral, durable, durable-dedup, durable-conversation), partition count, dedup TTL, and checkpoint policy.
DurableStore trait defined in crates/liminal/src/durability/store.rs with methods mapping to haematite's append, read_from, cas, and scan operations.
HaematiteStore struct implements DurableStore by delegating to haematite's EventStore API.
durability/mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic.

stories.text: As an application developer, I want to choose between dedup and durable-conversation exactly-once strategies per channel so that simple consumers use the lightweight approach and complex consumers use the heavyweight one.
As the Aion engine, I want the durability layer to use haematite's append/cas API directly so that workflow state and channel state share the same storage engine without an additional dependency.

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines. If a module approaches this limit, extract into submodules.
No panics in production code. .unwrap() and .expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains ONLY pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic, no type definitions, no function bodies.
All public types implement Debug. All error types implement std::error::Error via thiserror.
Every haematite append call must include an expected_seq for optimistic concurrency. No blind appends.
Consumer cursor checkpoint must use cas (compare-and-swap), never unconditional write. A stale checkpoint must fail, not silently regress.
Durable conversation replay must be idempotent: replaying the same event log twice produces identical state.
Ephemeral channels must not hold a reference to the DurableStore. The type system must prevent accidental persistence calls on ephemeral channels.
Dedup TTL expiry must never delete a receipt that is younger than the configured TTL, even under clock skew up to 1 second.
All durability operations must propagate haematite errors as DurabilityError variants, never swallow or log-and-continue.

resolved_context.intention:
When this cluster is complete, a developer can declare a channel as durable and get message persistence, replay from offset, and crash recovery without changing their publish/subscribe code. A developer building complex processing can choose a durable conversation and get exactly-once semantics that are emergent from resumable processing — not from delivery guarantees and not from application-level idempotency boilerplate.

The durability layer should feel like a channel property, not a separate system. Setting a channel to durable adds persistence; setting it to ephemeral bypasses haematite entirely with zero overhead. The two exactly-once strategies (lightweight dedup contract and heavyweight durable conversations) are complementary tools chosen per-channel, not competing approaches forced on all consumers.

The bus's relationship with haematite is direct and mechanical: append for writes, cas for cursors, read for replay. No ORM, no query builder, no abstraction that hides what haematite is doing. Developers who understand haematite's append/read/cas model understand the durability layer.

provenance.quote: When this cluster is complete, a developer can declare a channel as durable and get message persistence, replay from offset, and crash recovery without changing their publish/subscribe code. A develop
